headline: ten integrations ≠ one shared context
sub: An agent is only as good as what it reads. One canonical source, ~60% fewer tokens per lookup on our repo.

headline: stale hand-written context rots
sub: An agent quoted a runbook we'd already deleted. trovex regenerates context from the repo, not from memory.
Your repo, your number. Install it, point it at a repo, read your own.

headline: wired the whole growth fleet to one shared board
sub: And the savings view prints the token receipt per run. Real numbers, in public.

headline: you can install the tool friday and still not be ai-native monday
sub: AI-native is an org rewire, not a SKU: where context lives, who reviews the output, how a task gets claimed.

headline: paste overtook refactor
sub: GitClear, 211M lines of code. In 2024, copy-paste overtook refactoring for the first time.

cols.head: BOUGHT SEATS
cols.body: licenses handed out. the workflow stayed exactly the same.

cols.head: REWIRED THE FLOW
cols.body: readable context, a named owner. that's where the gain is.

headline: you're only as ready as your weakest dimension
sub: Six of them: context, orchestration, observability, operating model, team trust, governance.
